markets.question: Will Trade Desk (TTD) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Trade Desk is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Trade Desk’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.32 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Trade Desk reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.32 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Trade Desk releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

events.title: Will Trade Desk (TTD) beat quarterly earnings?
events.description:
As of market creation, Trade Desk is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Trade Desk’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.32 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Trade Desk reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $0.32 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Trade Desk releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 83.5% implied probability that Trade Desk (TTD) will beat Q1 2026 earnings consensus of $0.32–$0.38 EPS and ~$679–$687 million revenue, reflecting the company's track record of meeting or exceeding revenue estimates in the last three quarters despite a Q2 2025 EPS miss. Conservative Q1 guidance—at least $678 million revenue and ~$195 million adjusted EBITDA—sets a low bar amid 10% year-over-year growth expectations, bolstered by 95%+ customer retention for 12 straight years and joint business plans now over half of revenue following April expansions. Downward analyst revisions (13 EPS cuts in past 30 days) are offset by share repurchases and programmatic ad strength; watch May 7 report for Kokai platform updates.

markets.question: Will the NYT front-page headlines say "Bitcoin" this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed term is included in a headline on the New York Times front page between April 27 and May 3, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A headline is defined as the bolded or enlarged text directly preceding each article, previewing the article’s content and typically separated from the article’s text by a black line and byline. The primary headline for each story is the headline for that story with the largest text, typically appearing in bold font and above any other headlines or text for that article.

Sub-headlines, defined as additional bolded or enlarged text not separated from the primary headline by any text, will count, whether they appear before the byline or are partially surrounded by the article text but still adjacent to the primary headline. Pull quotes, however, or any bolded text not adjacent to the primary headline, will not count.

Banner headlines, defined as front-page headlines bordered on the sides only by white space, will count.

Image captions, article text, or any other text that does not constitute a headline will not qualify.

Any plural or possessive forms of a listed term, as well as variations in capitalization, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will not count.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all slang forms, will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

If the listed term appears as part of a compound word, use of that compound word will qualify, provided the listed term remains a distinct component of the compound. This does not include suffixes, prefixes, alternative tenses, or grammatical variations that alter the root word. For example, if the listed term is “joy,” “killjoy” qualifies but “joyful” does not. If the listed term is “sun,” “sunflower” qualifies but “sunny” does not.

If the listed term is part of a hyphenated compound, use of that hyphenated compound will qualify. For example, if the listed term is “NATO,” “pro-NATO” and “anti-NATO” qualify.

If the listed term has non-standard transliterations into English, such transliterations will qualify if they are phonetically equivalent. For example, if the listed term is “Erdogan,” “Erdoğan” qualifies. If the listed term is “Zelensky,” “Zelenskiy” qualifies.

If the listed term is an abbreviation, periodized forms of that abbreviation will qualify. For example, if the listed term is “AI,” “A.I.” qualifies. However, extraneous symbols inserted into a word (e.g. r@d1cal for “radical”) will disqualify it from counting toward a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be the New York Times daily newspaper, including images of the front page posted daily at: https://nytimes.pressreader.com/the-new-york-times/.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the NYT front-page headlines market centers on escalating U.S.-Iran tensions, which have dominated coverage since ceasefire talks collapsed over the weekend, prompting headlines on Iran's new missile reveal and a snag in Trump-Xi summit plans over an Iran blockade reported May 1. The White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting—where a gunman was charged with attempted presidential assassination—shocked the news cycle starting April 27, alongside King Charles's U.S. visit amid delicate geopolitics and Supreme Court arguments. With print editions through May 3 unresolved, traders eye Friday-Saturday developments like potential Iran escalations or royal tour updates, as NYT prioritizes high-stakes foreign policy and security crises in its agenda-setting front-page selections.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the CA-26 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CA-26 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Incumbent Julia Brownley's January retirement opened the race, but a crowded Democratic primary field—led by well-funded state Sen. Jacqui Irwin with over $556,000 raised—dominates over fragmented Republicans like Samuel Gallucci, whose fundraising lags significantly per March FEC filings. With the top-two nonpartisan primary looming on June 2, markets anticipate two Democrats advancing to November's general election. Upsets could arise from a GOP consolidation for second place, a Democratic scandal, or national midterm dynamics shifting turnout in Ventura County.

markets.question: Blackburn Rovers FC vs. Leicester City FC: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming EFL Championship game between Blackburn Rovers FC and Leicester City FC, scheduled for May 2 at 7:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Blackburn Rovers FC and Leicester City FC combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Blackburn Rovers FC vs. Leicester City FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the EFL Championship game, scheduled for May 2 at 7:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In a pivotal EFL Championship relegation six-pointer at Ewood Park, Blackburn Rovers (19th, 46 points) host bottom-of-the-table Leicester City (22nd, 40 points after a six-point deduction) on May 2, with both sides desperate for points in the final stretch. Blackburn hold a slight edge from a recent 1-0 away win at Birmingham on April 3, extending an unbeaten run of five matches (two wins, three draws), bolstered by Kristi Montgomery's injury return. Leicester, managed by Gary Rowett, salvaged a 2-2 draw at home to Preston last weekend but sit one point from safety amid a winless streak in five, compounded by midfielder Jordan James' Achilles doubt and longer-term absences like Harry Souttar. Rovers' prior 2-0 victory over the Foxes in November underscores home advantage and head-to-head momentum in this survival scrap.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty that Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will not shake hands by June 30, driven by entrenched disagreements over territorial withdrawals, annexed regions, and full Russian troop pullouts amid ongoing military stalemate. Recent developments, including Putin's conditional openness to a meeting only for finalizing agreements (April 22) and proposals for a short-term May 9 Victory Day ceasefire discussed with U.S. President Trump (late April), have yielded no progress toward direct talks, with Zelenskyy seeking details but rejecting key Russian demands. Absent a major diplomatic breakthrough—such as successful U.S.-mediated negotiations or de-escalation signals—the timeline leaves insufficient room for personal diplomacy.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a fragmented field for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, with Yulia Navalnaya's 8.5% implied probability edging Volodymyr Zelenskyy at 7.8% and Donald Trump at 6.5%, driven by yesterday's Norwegian Nobel Committee disclosure of 287 nominees—208 individuals and 79 organizations. Navalnaya gains from her role as a symbol of Russian opposition post-Alexei Navalny, Zelenskyy from a publicized early April nomination by an Oslo professor alongside Ukraine's people for defending democracy, and Trump from reported endorsements by Cambodia, Israel, and Pakistan leaders citing diplomacy. This tight race underscores competing global narratives around Ukraine resistance, authoritarian challenges, and Middle East tensions, with no frontrunner momentum amid the committee's secretive process; odds could shift on late-breaking peace breakthroughs before the October 9 announcement.

markets.question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be less than $500B at market close on IPO day by December 31, 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2027".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 220-239 tweets from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters implied probabilities tightly around 140-199 posts for Elon Musk's X activity from April 28 to May 5, with 160-179 leading at 23.9%, reflecting his established weekly cadence of 20-30 posts daily amid fluctuating news cycles. Recent xAI's Grok 4.3 launch—topping benchmarks in law and agentic tasks—has fueled viral engagement, alongside Musk's commentary on immigration, voter ID, and Neuralink demos, while a California regulator apology to SpaceX on April 29 added promotional fodder. Yet, xtracker shows just 51 posts at 57% elapsed (daily pace ~14), slowed by his OpenAI trial testimony; acceleration hinges on weekend political or Tesla catalysts before May 5 resolution.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Melanie Stansbury (D) commands trader consensus at 91% implied probability to win New Mexico's 1st Congressional District, reflecting the seat's solid Democratic lean—demonstrated by strong partisan voting index and Harris's 2024 margin—and her fundraising dominance over Republican nominee Ndidiamaka "Didi" Okpareke, a pharmacist who secured the unopposed GOP nod in March. With no public polling indicating competitiveness and primaries set for June 2, the race aligns with historical incumbency advantages in safe districts. Challenges would require a Stansbury scandal, massive GOP national midterm wave, or Okpareke fundraising surge, though structural barriers remain high.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Europe at 72.5% implied probability to claim the 2026 FIFA World Cup title, driven by UEFA's 16 qualifiers including powerhouses Spain, France, England, Germany, Portugal, and Netherlands—top-ranked contenders per recent power rankings—bolstered by historical dominance with the last two winners from the continent. South America's 21% reflects CONMEBOL's strong six-team contingent of reigning champions Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Ecuador, Paraguay, and Uruguay, maintaining elite depth despite fewer slots. Late March UEFA play-offs secured solid additions like Bosnia and Herzegovina (upsetting Italy), Sweden, Türkiye, and Czechia, while inter-confederation wins for Iraq (AFC) and DR Congo (CAF) failed to elevate challengers; Asia, Africa, North America, and Oceania trail with unproven title pedigrees amid the expanded 48-team group stage draw.

Sinner's dominant clay swing, including his first Monte-Carlo Masters 1000 title earlier this month and a perfect 9-0 record on the surface in 2026, underscores his improved baseline game and serving prowess on Roland Garros' red clay. Alexander Zverev (6.2%) follows as a proven clay finalist with strong home-stretch form, while Novak Djokovic (5.9%) holds veteran appeal despite age. Rising Frenchman Arthur Fils (5.3%) and Spaniard Rafael Jodar (3.5%), who recently challenged Sinner in Madrid, represent dark-horse threats amid a wide-open field.

markets.question: Will SpaceX raise less than $40B in its IPO?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total gross proceeds raised by SpaceX in its initial public offering (IPO) at the time of pricing.

The amount raised is defined as the aggregate dollar value of shares sold at the final offering price at the time of IPO pricing, as disclosed in the final prospectus filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares sold pursuant to any overallotment option (greenshoe) will not be considered.

Secondary sales of existing shares by current shareholders will be included only to the extent that they are part of the IPO offering at pricing and reflected in the total gross proceeds. Private secondary transactions conducted outside of the IPO will not be considered.

If the total proceeds fall exactly on a boundary between two ranges, this market will resolve to the higher range.

If SpaceX does not complete an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

The primary resolution source will be the final prospectus filed with the SEC; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects deep uncertainty in SpaceX's IPO raise amount, with market-implied probabilities nearly split at 49.5% for both $50-60 billion and $80-90 billion outcomes following the company's confidential S-1 filing on April 1 and subsequent details targeting a $75 billion raise at a $1.75-2 trillion valuation for a late-June listing. This contest stems from Starlink's subscriber inflection—projected to drive revenue beyond $16 billion in 2025—bolstering bull case demand against valuation skepticism, as the implied multiple exceeds 100x EBITDA amid xAI integration and Mars ambitions. Recent tender offers valued the firm at $800 billion, highlighting aggressive growth pricing, while broader equity volatility could force pricing adjustments. Key swing factors include early June roadshow reception and final share allocation, with historical mega-IPOs like Meta's $16 billion underscoring execution risks.

Thicknesse's slick striking (3.33 SLpM) and jiu-jitsu from Volkanovski's camp contrast Morales' power punching (3.57 SLpM, 70" reach edge) amid the American's four-fight UFC skid via decisions to Raul Rosas Jr., Elijah Smith, and others, plus high strike absorption (4.00 SApM). Both made weight cleanly yesterday—no injury concerns—with stylistic clash favoring grappling control in a competitive bout.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors no Trump-Putin meeting by June 30 (84%), driven by the absence of any announced in-person summit plans despite their first 2026 phone call on April 29, where they discussed a temporary Ukraine ceasefire tied to Russia's May 9 Victory Day and Putin's offer to mediate in the Iran conflict. President Trump's invitation for Putin to the G20 summit in Miami in December falls well beyond the resolution date. Persistent Ukraine war stalemate, U.S. sanctions, and Putin's ICC warrant limit viable neutral venues like China (5%) or Gulf states, with traders pricing slim odds on Russia-hosted talks amid diplomatic caution. Zelenskyy's requests for ceasefire details signal ongoing multilateral talks but no bilateral summit momentum.

markets.question: Spread: SC Freiburg (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 3 at 1:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "SC Freiburg" if SC Freiburg win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "VfL Wolfsburg".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SC Freiburg vs. VfL Wolfsburg - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Bundesliga game, scheduled for May 3 at 1:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: SC Freiburg holds a strong position in eighth place, riding a solid run of three recent wins and aiming to solidify mid-table security with home advantage at Europa-Park Stadion against relegation-threatened VfL Wolfsburg, who sit 17th amid a winless streak and just one victory in their last five matches. Wolfsburg's woes deepened yesterday with captain Maximilian Arnold ruled out due to a lingering groin injury, joining key absences like Jonas Wind (hamstring), Jenson Seelt (knee), and Kevin Paredes (muscle), thinning their squad for this survival scrap. Freiburg boasts balanced head-to-head history (14 wins to Wolfsburg's 13) and robust home form, while Wolfsburg's away struggles and injury crisis tilt trader consensus toward the hosts in this penultimate matchday clash.

markets.question: Will Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) hit (HIGH) $435 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.MSFT%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Traders eye May volatility from tech sector rotation, inflation data, and FOMC signals ahead of June's Build conference and Q4 earnings.

events.title: Solana above ___ on May 2?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 22°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's official observations confirm the highest temperature on April 30, 2026, reached 26.7°C, driving unanimous trader consensus with 100% implied probability for 25°C or higher. This outcome reflects an exceptionally warm April marked by persistent southerly airstreams and anticyclonic aloft conditions that fueled above-normal maxima, including a monthly peak of 30.2°C on April 16 amid abundant sunshine. Late spring climatology in the region routinely delivers daily highs exceeding 25°C, aligning with observed patterns despite a cloudier end to the month under northeast monsoon influences. Post-observation certainty is near-absolute, with only improbable data revisions from official measurements posing any challenge.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in London be 15°C on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors 18°C (36.5% implied probability) as London's highest temperature on May 3, closely trailed by 19°C (27%) and 17°C (22.5%), reflecting Met Office forecasts projecting a maximum of 19°C amid light rain and 90% precipitation chance. This shift follows a warm spell peaking at 25°C on May 1 and 23°C on May 2 under sunny intervals, but an incoming frontal system introduces cooler, moist air masses, extensive cloud cover, and possible thunderstorms, capping insolation and surface heating. Model ensembles show uncertainty in shower timing and cloud opacity—less persistent overcast could push toward 19°C, while heavier rain favors 17°C. Watch for Saturday evening updates as observations refine guidance ahead of bank holiday weekend resolution.

markets.question: Will Khvicha Kvaratskhelia be the 2025/2026 top UCL goal scorer?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the top goalscorer of the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League.

If multiple players tie for the most goals in the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League season, the market will resolve based on most assists, then most games played, and finally the player's last name alphabetically if ties persist.

If a player's team is eliminated, and they are not in the goal lead, the market for that player may resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA Champions League. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Harry Kane trails closely with 13 goals in 12 appearances for semifinalist Bayern, including key strikes in recent quarterfinal wins over Real and the thrilling 5-4 first-leg semi loss to Paris Saint-Germain on April 28, fueling his 41% standing as the primary challenger. Trailing players like Khvicha Kvaratskhelia and Julián Álvarez, both on 10 goals for semi contenders PSG and Atlético Madrid, hold negligible odds below 1% given the need for extraordinary output in upcoming second legs and potential final to surpass the leaders.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls, including a Media Taehee-Media Nal survey showing Democratic Party candidate Shin Yong-han at 49.5% against incumbent People Power Party Governor Kim Young-hwan's 38.7%—outside the margin of error—have solidified trader consensus on Shin's commanding lead in the June 3 Chungcheongbuk-do gubernatorial election. Earlier April surveys from Yeoronjosa Kkot echoed this, with Shin ahead 51.2% to 30.9%, driven by his April 4 primary victory over Noh Yeong-min and momentum from Democratic Party support amid Kim's turbulent nomination process involving a court-suspended cut-off. While incumbency typically favors re-election in South Korean local races, these trends reflect Shin's strength in key areas like Cheongju and among 40-60-year-olds. Barring scandals, gaffes, or national political shifts, the race appears decisively tilted.

markets.question: Meta (META) Up or Down on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Close price for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) on May 1, 2026 is higher than the Close price for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the Close price for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) on May 1, 2026 is lower than the Close price for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless that Friday were a market holiday, in which case it would refer to Thursday, or the next most recent trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Closing prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) does not trade at all during the regular session, the market will resolve 50-50.

For a standard full trading session, the closing price refers to the Pyth "Close" value of the 1-minute candle corresponding to the final minute of regular trading hours on the primary exchange.

If either of the relevant days has no valid Pyth Close value for the 1-minute candle corresponding to the end of regular trading hours on the primary exchange, the market will use the last valid Pyth price achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange as the effective closing price. If no valid Pyth price exists for that trading day due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption, the official closing price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine the closing price for that day.

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed security during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market will be Pyth, specifically the "Close" values for the relevant 1-minute candle available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.META%2FUSD?t=1773432000).

markets.question: Will Hunter Brown strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Houlahan, an Air Force veteran seeking a fifth term, secured 56% in 2024 and larger margins previously against underfunded challengers, buoyed by Chester County's suburban, college-educated voters who have trended reliably blue. With March filing deadlines passed, her May 19 primary faces no serious opposition, while Republican Marty Young enters without national buzz or fundraising edge. Odds hold firm absent a Houlahan scandal, primary upset, or strong national Republican midterm wave tilting battleground Pennsylvania.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tarik Skubal leads trader consensus at 21.5% implied probability for the 2026 AL Cy Young thanks to his back-to-back wins in 2024-25 and a strong early-season 2.70 ERA with a 0.95 WHIP over seven starts for the Tigers, including recent quality outings despite not yet peaking. Jose Soriano (12.6%) surges on a microscopic 0.84 ERA and 5-1 record for the Angels through eight starts—49 strikeouts despite a rare hiccup last Tuesday—highlighting his transition to ace status. Yankees rookie Cam Schlittler (12.4%) dazzles with a 1.51 ERA, 4-1 mark, and elite 0.74 WHIP in 41.2 innings, fueling his rapid rise via fastball command. Dylan Cease (12.0%) benefits from his offseason move to Toronto's rotation, offering strikeout upside amid a wide-open field defined by April's small-sample volatility.

markets.question: Will an Iranian agent be charged in the US by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of any individual alleged to be acting as an agent of the Iranian government between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Iranian agent” refers to any individual alleged to be acting on behalf of, under the direction of, or in coordination with the Iranian government (including its military or intelligence services) to conduct espionage, collect intelligence, or perform other covert operations that benefit Iran.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

Subsequent developments, like the April 24 unsealing of migrant smuggling charges against Jafar Tafakori, lack allegations of government direction. With three weeks remaining and no announced investigations or hearings, the sporadic pace of prior cases, such as the March conviction of Asif Merchant in a murder-for-hire plot, underpins skepticism for another timely indictment amid ongoing U.S. sanctions enforcement.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's official observations confirm the highest temperature on April 30, 2026, reached 26.7°C, driving unanimous trader consensus with 100% implied probability for 25°C or higher. Late spring climatology in the region routinely delivers daily highs exceeding 25°C, aligning with observed patterns despite a cloudier end to the month under northeast monsoon influences. Post-observation certainty is near-absolute, with only improbable data revisions from official measurements posing any challenge.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $304 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's recent threats to withdraw the United States from NATO, amid allies' refusal to join the US-Israel military action against Iran, have fueled speculation, with White House spokeswoman Caroline Leavitt confirming internal discussions as recently as early April 2026. However, trader consensus reflects deep skepticism due to statutory barriers in Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act, which prohibits unilateral presidential withdrawal without congressional approval or a two-thirds Senate vote. Bipartisan opposition from senators like Tim Kaine and Republicans underscores institutional resistance, while NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte deemed an exit unlikely in mid-April. No formal steps have advanced, and upcoming congressional sessions or diplomatic summits could clarify commitments before any 2027 deadline.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve's April 29, 2026 FOMC statement maintained the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% for the third straight meeting, reflecting trader consensus on prolonged higher-for-longer policy amid March CPI inflation accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—and an 8-4 voting split signaling internal caution. Resilient labor markets and sticky services prices have diminished expectations for aggressive easing, with 10-year Treasury yields holding near 4.20% and CME FedWatch implying low odds of a June rate cut. Polymarket positions aggregate this skin-in-the-game sentiment, pricing limited downside in the fed funds rate before 2027. Key catalysts include April CPI data on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC, which could recalibrate the market-implied rate path.

markets.question: Lakers vs. Rockets
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 1 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Lakers win, the market will resolve to "Lakers".
If the Rockets win, the market will resolve to "Rockets".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lakers hold a 3-2 series lead over the Rockets in their 2026 NBA Western Conference first-round playoff matchup, with Game 6 tonight at Houston's Toyota Center. The Rockets staged a stunning rally from an 0-3 deficit, dominating Game 4 (115-96) and grinding out a road win in Game 5 (99-93) by forcing 15 Lakers turnovers worth 18 points, leveraging their second-youngest postseason starting lineup ever—Reed Sheppard, Jabari Smith Jr., Amen Thompson, Alperen Sengun, and Tari Eason. Kevin Durant remains doubtful for Houston with an ankle sprain since Game 2, while Lakers' Austin Reaves returned from a four-week oblique absence to drop 22 points off the bench in Game 5; Luka Doncic is out with a hamstring strain. Lakers' ball security and LeBron James' finishing face Rockets' full-court pressure and home-court edge in a potential closeout or Game 7-forcing battle.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Shevchenko by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Shevchenko, Donetsk Oblast, (48.384157880601364° N, 37.10809761679785° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

Geolocated reports confirm Russian infiltration attempts into nearby Rodynske and flanking maneuvers from Hryshyne, but Ukrainian defenses have inflicted severe casualties, preventing entry into Shevchenko itself and maintaining control over key high ground that disrupts enemy logistics. This slow progress, despite intensified assaults in the Dobropillia sector, underpins trader consensus on limited near-term capture risks. Upcoming developments, including potential Ukrainian reinforcements or Russian escalations ahead of improved spring mobility, could shift frontline dynamics before any resolution deadline.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Joe Wilson's dominant fundraising—$294,000 cash on hand as of late March—and history of double-digit general election victories, including 59.5% in 2024, anchor trader consensus at 82% implied probability for a Republican Party win in the safely red SC-02 district, rated Solid Republican by Cook Political Report with an R+7 partisan lean where Trump won by 14 points last year. The June 9 Republican primary pits Wilson against challengers Hamp Redmond and Sam Gibbons, but his incumbency and prior 73.9% primary margin position him as heavy favorite. Democrats hold 18% amid a fragmented primary field featuring 2024 nominee David Robinson II, Zyon Khalifa, Roger Pruitt, and Daniel Shrief, with no recent polling or catalysts post-March 30 filing deadline to shift dynamics ahead of the November 3 general election. Close pricing on leading candidate-specific outcomes A, B, and Other around 50% reflects nominee uncertainty.

This positioning stems from persistent moderate onshore pressure gradients fueling a 2500-foot-deep marine layer, producing patchy fog before 11 a.m. PDT and gradual clearing thereafter, which caps daytime heating under stratocumulus clouds. The latest Area Forecast Discussion, updated at 1:12 p.m. PDT on May 1, notes slight cooling from Friday's coastal highs (67-75°F) due to lower 500 mb heights, with model persistence supporting mid-to-upper 60s near the coast. Nearby 70-71°F (28.5%) and 66-67°F (27.5%) reflect uncertainty in clearing timing and gusty afternoon west-southwest winds up to 20 mph; watch hourly NWS observations for resolution.

markets.question: Will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit (HIGH) $150 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD trades around 1.173 as of May 1, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on persistent monetary policy divergence, with the ECB holding its deposit rate at 2.00% after April's decision amid eurozone inflation hitting 3% and tepid growth, while the Fed maintains the federal funds rate at 3.50%–3.75%. This setup supports modest euro strength year-to-date from January lows near 1.16, driven by market-implied odds of ECB hikes starting June versus potential Fed easing later. Key risks include upcoming ECB June 11 meeting, Eurozone CPI on May 7, and US nonfarm payrolls May 2, alongside geopolitical tensions influencing risk appetite and Treasury yields near 4.20%. Prediction markets price euro upside to 1.18–1.20 by year-end under rate convergence scenarios.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve's April 29, 2026 FOMC statement maintained the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% for the third straight meeting, reflecting trader consensus on prolonged higher-for-longer policy amid March CPI inflation accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—and an 8-4 voting split signaling internal caution. Resilient labor markets and sticky services prices have diminished expectations for aggressive easing, with 10-year Treasury yields holding near 4.20% and CME FedWatch implying low odds of a June rate cut. Polymarket positions aggregate this skin-in-the-game sentiment, pricing limited downside in the fed funds rate before 2027. Key catalysts include April CPI data on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC, which could recalibrate the market-implied rate path.

markets.question: Chelsea FC vs. Nottingham Forest FC: Draw at halftime?
markets.description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 4, 2026 If the game ends in a draw within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No". This market refers only to the outcome within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Chelsea FC vs. Nottingham Forest FC - Halftime Result
events.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Chelsea FC and Nottingham Forest FC, scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET: This event contains halftime result markets for home, draw, and away outcomes within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin contest for the halftime result in Chelsea's home Premier League clash with Nottingham Forest, with all outcomes at 50% amid widespread injury doubts on both sides. Chelsea, sitting sixth in the table, face defensive headaches without Reece James, Levi Colwill, and Trevoh Chalobah, while João Pedro and Estevão remain fitness concerns after recent thigh and hamstring issues; Enzo Fernández is expected back from a calf knock. Forest, buoyed by Europa League semifinal progress but ninth in standings, counter with their own absences including Chris Wood, Callum Hudson-Odoi, and Murillo, yet boast resilient away form that has frustrated top sides. Cautious opening exchanges in prior head-to-heads further even the implied probabilities, highlighting lineup uncertainties and mutual vulnerabilities.

markets.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 93% implied probability against direct talks between North and South Korea by June 30, driven by Pyongyang's entrenched hostility and prioritization of military advancements over dialogue. North Korea's multiple short-range ballistic missile tests on April 19 underscored ongoing escalation, while Kim Jong Un's March formalization of South Korea as its "primary hostile state" remains the dominant policy framework, blocking bilateral engagement. Recent high-level visits from Russian State Duma Chairman Vyacheslav Volodin on April 27 and Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on April 10 highlight Pyongyang's deepening alliances with Moscow and Beijing, diverting focus from Seoul. South Korea's early April apology for a drone incursion elicited a mild North Korean response but no reciprocal overtures or scheduled negotiations, reinforcing structural barriers absent late-breaking diplomatic shifts.

markets.question: Will Israel win the televote for Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:
The Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 is scheduled for May 16, 2026.

This market will resolve to the country that receives the highest number of points from the televote in the Grand Final of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026.

All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israel leads Polymarket's Eurovision 2026 televote winner odds at 33% implied probability, buoyed by Noam Bettan's multilingual "Michelle"—a heartfelt entry with Hebrew, French, and English lyrics—that surged in betting markets after its March release amid Israel's resilient national final selection and historical diaspora voting strength. Greece's Akylas follows closely at 24.5% with the dynamic "Ferto," praised for its stylistic shifts and vibrant London Eurovision Party performance, fueling fan buzz in recent OGAE votes. Finland's violin-pop duo Linda Lampenius x Pete Parkkonen ("Liekinheitin") holds 19.5%, dominating overall frontrunner polls but seen as jury-leaning, creating tight televote contention. First rehearsals kick off this week in Vienna, where stage presence and production could swing trader consensus before semis on May 12 and 14.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Elon+Bull+Run+Parlay.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Elon Bull Run Parlay, with "No" at an 88% implied probability, reflects the compounded unlikelihood of all three 2026 milestones aligning: Elon Musk reaching trillionaire status per Bloomberg Billionaires Index, announcing another child, and SpaceX achieving 9+ Starship launches to 100 km altitude. As of early May, Musk's net worth hovers around $800 billion—buoyed by Tesla's $573 million in sales to xAI and SpaceX but far from doubling amid volatile markets—while no new baby has been announced. Starship progress lags with just one qualifying flight (Flight 12 on April 7), despite Flight 13 slated for early May and FAA approvals for up to 44 launches annually; historical cadence and technical hurdles temper expectations for the required pace. Recent Tesla-xAI investments signal synergies, but traders see slim odds for the full parlay by year-end.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 50.5% implied probability for South Korea's 2026 annual inflation exceeding 3.0%, driven by March 2026 CPI rising to 2.2% year-over-year—up from 2.0% in prior months—alongside surging producer prices at a three-year high of 4.1% and inflation expectations climbing to 2.9% in April. Upward forecast revisions from IMF to 2.5%, AMRO to 2.3%, and others reflect global energy price risks and potential unwind of oil price caps suppressing CPI by 0.4–0.8 percentage points. Bank of Korea's April policy rate hold at 2.5% signals mid-to-high 2% inflation outlook amid robust semiconductor exports bolstering growth. Buckets from 2.1–2.9% capture 25–28% each, with April CPI due May 6 as next catalyst.
